mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS change and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises more than 0.5 percentage points above its trailing 12-month low, a Sahm-rule-style labor deterioration signal activates, indicating recession risk and predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations reprice downward.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises > 0.5pp above its trailing 12-month low
failure_condition: In periods where the Sahm-style trigger fires, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the nominal FEDFUNDS rate exceeds the trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate, implying a positive and rising real policy rate, tightening financial conditions compress equity multiples; the joint configuration of high FEDFUNDS and decelerating CPIAUCSL growth predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, an effect not captured by either series alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate > 1.5pp
failure_condition: When the real-rate trigger is active, forward 20d SPX returns show no statistically significant negative mean (t-test p > 0.05) versus untriggered periods over a 3-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a sustained decline in HOUST (housing starts) signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand that propagates to broader consumption and corporate earnings with a lag, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter specifically when FEDFUNDS is elevated.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 10% over trailing 6 months while FEDFUNDS > 4%
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns following triggered HOUST declines during tight-money regimes are not significantly lower than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and forward corporate profitability, generating a short-horizon positive drift in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets incrementally price in improving supply-side fundamentals.
failure_condition: Rank IC between monthly INDPRO change and forward 5d SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation crosses above 3%, markets anticipate sustained restrictive monetary policy, raising the discount rate applied to equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days until inflation decelerates back below the threshold.
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns during periods with CPIAUCSL YoY > 3% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) across a 3-year walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in PAYEMS monthly growth combined with a rising UNRATE level signals a turning labor market cycle that leading equity markets have not yet fully priced, predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; neither series alone carries this predictive power because PAYEMS deceleration without UNRATE confirmation often reflects noise.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth falls below 1% while UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over trailing 3 months
failure_condition: When both conditions co-trigger, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or joint-signal Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, an unexpected acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) above trend signals resilient consumer demand that supports near-term corporate revenue beats, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days, though the effect is muted or reversed once the expansion matures into overheating.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS month-over-month acceleration and forward 5d SPX returns falls below 0.03 during identified late-cycle expansion windows over a rolling 2-year sample.
